description: Production-ready AI Chat SaaS Starter Kit — Next.js 15 + Supabase + Vercel AI SDK + Stripe. Clone, configure, deploy. Ship your AI product in hours.
targetMarket: Global developers & entrepreneurs who want to build AI chat products
designTheme: Dark + AI Gradient Glow (#09090b bg, #141419 cards, #7c3aed→#2563eb→#06b6d4 gradient, glassmorphism)

userStories.title: Project scaffold & base config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to clone ChatKit and have a working Next.js project with all dependencies installed, so that I can start developing immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Next.js 15 project with App Router and TypeScript strict mode
All npm dependencies installed (supabase, ai sdk, stripe, zustand, framer-motion, resend, etc.)
Tailwind CSS v4 configured with dark theme colors
shadcn/ui initialized with base components
Geist font (Sans + Mono) configured in layout
globals.css with design tokens: --background, --card, --primary, --gradient-ai, glassmorphism, glow effects
.env.example with all required env vars documented
Folder structure: app/(auth), app/(chat), app/(landing), components/, lib/, utils/
`npm run build` passes with zero errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented and verified - build passes, typecheck passes

userStories.title: Supabase database schema & migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the database schema pre-configured with tables for profiles, conversations, messages, usage, and subscriptions, so that I don't have to design the schema from scratch.
acceptanceCriteria: profiles table: id (uuid, FK to auth.users), display_name, avatar_url, plan (free/pro/enterprise), stripe_customer_id, created_at, updated_at
conversations table: id (uuid), user_id (FK), title (text), model (text), system_prompt (text), created_at, updated_at
messages table: id (uuid), conversation_id (FK), role (enum: user/assistant/system), content (text), tokens_input (int), tokens_output (int), model (text), created_at
usage table: id (uuid), user_id (FK), model (text), tokens_input (int), tokens_output (int), created_at
subscriptions table: id (uuid), user_id (FK), stripe_subscription_id, plan (text), status (text), current_period_start, current_period_end, cancel_at_period_end (bool)
RLS policies: users can only CRUD their own data
Indexes on user_id, conversation_id, created_at for query performance
SQL migration file in supabase/migrations/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented and verified - build passes, typecheck passes

userStories.title: Supabase client helpers & middleware
userStories.description: As a developer, I want pre-configured Supabase client helpers for server, browser, and middleware, so that auth works seamlessly with Next.js App Router.
acceptanceCriteria: utils/supabase/server.ts — createServerClient with cookie handling for Server Components
utils/supabase/client.ts — createBrowserClient for Client Components
utils/supabase/middleware.ts — session refresh in middleware
middleware.ts at root — refreshes auth session on every request, protects /chat and /settings routes
Redirect unauthenticated users from /chat/* to /login
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented and verified - build passes, typecheck passes

userStories.title: Authentication — Login page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sign in via email/password or OAuth (Google, GitHub), so that I can access my chat conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: Login page at /login with dark theme, ChatKit branding
Email + password form with validation (Zod schema)
'Continue with Google' button → Supabase OAuth
'Continue with GitHub' button → Supabase OAuth
'Don't have an account? Sign up' link to /register
Error states: invalid credentials, network error
Loading state on submit button
Redirect to /chat after successful login
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Auth with React Hook Form + Zod + Supabase Auth (email + Google + GitHub OAuth). Build passes.

userStories.title: Authentication — Register page
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to create an account via email/password or OAuth, so that I can start using ChatKit.
acceptanceCriteria: Register page at /register with dark theme, ChatKit branding
Email + password form with validation (Zod: email format, password min 8 chars)
'Sign up with Google' button → Supabase OAuth
'Sign up with GitHub' button → Supabase OAuth
'Already have an account? Sign in' link to /login
Auto-create profile row in Supabase after registration (trigger or server action)
Error states: email already exists, weak password
Redirect to /chat after successful registration
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Auth with React Hook Form + Zod + Supabase Auth (email + Google + GitHub OAuth). Build passes.

userStories.title: Chat UI — Sidebar with conversation list
userStories.description: As a user, I want a sidebar showing my conversations grouped by date, so that I can navigate between chats easily.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar component: 280px width, bg-card, border-right
Header with ChatKit logo (gradient-text) and '+' new chat button
Search input for filtering conversations
Conversation list grouped by: Today, Yesterday, Last 7 Days, Older
Each item shows: message icon + truncated title (max 28 chars)
Active conversation highlighted with bg-primary/10 and text-primary
Hover state: bg-background
Scrollable when conversations exceed viewport
Footer: user avatar + name + plan badge + settings link
Mobile (<768px): sidebar hidden, accessible via hamburger/sheet
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sidebar with real DB conversations, grouped by date (Today/Yesterday/7 Days/Older), search, rename, delete, user footer with logout. Build passes.

userStories.title: Chat UI — Message area with streaming
userStories.description: As a user, I want to chat with AI and see responses stream in real-time, so that I have a ChatGPT-like experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat area: flex-1, centered content (max-width 768px)
AI messages: sparkle avatar (32px, bg-primary/10, glow-ai), glass card bg, supports markdown
User messages: user avatar, bg-primary, text-primary-foreground, right-aligned
Code blocks: dark header bar with language label + copy button, syntax highlighting, gradient border
Streaming: tokens appear one-by-one using Vercel AI SDK useChat hook
Loading indicator while AI is generating (animated dots or shimmer)
Empty state for new conversation: centered greeting with AI sparkle icon
Auto-scroll to bottom on new message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Chat UI — Input field with model selector
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type messages and select which AI model to use, so that I can control my chat experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Input field: gradient-border wrapper, bg-card, rounded-xl, glow on focus
Placeholder: 'Message ChatKit...'
Send button: bg-primary, disabled when input empty
Submit on Enter (not Shift+Enter for newline)
Model selector dropdown in header: GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, Claude Sonnet, Gemini Flash
Selected model persists per conversation
Input clears after sending, focuses back
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Chat — Conversation management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create, rename, and delete conversations, so that I can organize my chat history.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new conversation: click '+' button or navigate to /chat/new
Auto-title: first 50 chars of first user message
Rename conversation: edit title via sidebar (click → inline edit)
Delete conversation: action menu → confirm dialog → delete from DB
Conversation list updates in real-time after changes
Deleting active conversation redirects to /chat/new
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Landing page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see a beautiful landing page that explains ChatKit's features and pricing, so that I decide to sign up.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero section: gradient-text heading, pill badge, CTA buttons (primary with glow + secondary with gradient-border)
Features grid: 6 feature cards (Multi-Model AI, Streaming, Auth, Stripe, Chat History, Premium UI) with icons
Tech stack section: pill badges for each technology
Pricing section: 3 tiers (Free $0, Pro $19, Enterprise $49) — Pro highlighted with gradient-border + glow
Navbar: logo, nav links, login, get started button. Glass effect on scroll
Footer: copyright, links
Fully responsive (mobile stacked, desktop grid)
Dark theme with AI gradient accents
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Settings page — Profile & API keys
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage my profile and configure AI API keys, so that I can personalize my experience and use my own API keys.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page at /settings with back navigation
Profile section: avatar (64px with gradient ring), display name, email, edit button
API Keys section: input fields for OpenAI, Anthropic, Google keys (masked by default)
Plan & Usage section: current plan badge, usage bar (messages used / limit)
Upgrade button linking to Stripe billing (or /settings/billing)
Delete account button (destructive, with confirm dialog)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Stripe billing integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Stripe billing pre-configured with subscription management, so that I can charge users for premium features.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/stripe.ts with Stripe client initialization
Stripe checkout: server action to create checkout session for Pro/Enterprise plans
Stripe webhook handler at /api/stripe/webhook for subscription events
Webhook events handled: checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted
Subscription status synced to Supabase subscriptions table
Stripe Customer Portal for managing billing at /settings/billing
Usage limits enforced: Free=10 msg/day, Pro=unlimited, Enterprise=unlimited+teams
Usage counter incremented on each AI message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AI SDK integration — Multi-model support
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Vercel AI SDK configured for multiple AI providers, so that end-users can switch between AI models seamlessly.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/ai.ts with model registry: GPT-4o, GPT-4o mini, Claude Sonnet, Gemini Flash
API route at /api/chat using Vercel AI SDK streamText
Model selected via request body, defaults to GPT-4o mini for Free users
Token counting: input and output tokens tracked per message
Error handling: rate limit, quota exceeded, invalid API key
Streaming response with proper content-type headers
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Documentation — README & setup guide
userStories.description: As a buyer, I want comprehensive documentation, so that I can set up and customize ChatKit easily.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md with: project overview, live demo link, screenshot, tech stack table, features list, quick start guide, project structure, customization guide, deployment guide, license
.env.example with all required and optional env vars documented with comments
SUPABASE_SETUP.md with step-by-step Supabase project + database setup
STRIPE_SETUP.md with Stripe configuration guide
All docs in clear, professional English
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Marketing assets — Thumbnails, cover, screenshots
userStories.description: As the seller, I want professional marketing assets, so that the Gumroad listing looks appealing and converts visitors.
acceptanceCriteria: Thumbnail: 1280x1280px, dark theme, ChatKit branding, AI gradient accents
Cover image: 1920x600px, dark theme, product name + tagline + gradient
OG image: 1200x630px for social sharing
12-16 screenshots of all pages: landing, chat, login, register, settings, pricing (dark + mobile)
All assets in public/ directory
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Gumroad listing & packaging
userStories.description: As the seller, I want the product packaged and listed on Gumroad, so that buyers can purchase and download it.
acceptanceCriteria: Source code packaged as ZIP (excluding node_modules, .next, .env.local)
Gumroad product created with: title, description, price tiers, cover, thumbnail, screenshots
Product description includes: features, tech stack, what's included, setup time, live demo link
Buyer receives ZIP file on purchase (tested)
Typecheck passes
